slices.focus: Presenting corridor results to senior leadership
slices.summary: Briefed senior staff on corridor program results, showing that reducing post-approval dropout — not increasing workshop attendance — was what actually drove mitigation adoption. Used dropout-stage data and resident photos to reframe success from activity counts to completion outcomes. Felt tired but vindicated after years of meticulous work finally validated. Plans to write a one-page internal note to preserve the core lesson before it gets flattened into a simplified headline.
open_loops: Whether the one-pager will effectively prevent the story from being flattened or diluted over time
How to share credit with Sharon and Miguel without diluting the core operational lesson that made the difference
What this new visibility and standing means internally for career trajectory — is this a one-off or a role shift
decisions: Draft a one-page internal note while the briefing is fresh to preserve the core operational lesson
Use dropout-stage reduction as the opening frame for future presentations rather than burying it mid-slide
Keep the explanation plain and jargon-free so the insight travels beyond the briefing room
